commands.review_summary:
The PR collapses denied plugin-harness tool policy into empty runtime allowlists, disables Codex app-server native tool/MCP/app surfaces for restricted runs, preserves existing tool-capable thread bindings, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lets toolsAllow: [] pass through as all Codex dynamic tools and always enables native code mode. I did not run a live Telegram/Codex reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is redacted live Telegram/EVE proof for a security-sensitive policy fix, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to attempt automatically.

target.title: Fix denied Codex app-server tool policy
target.body:
## Summary
- Honor deny-all (`deny: ["*"]`) and restricted runtime tool policies in the Codex app-server path by disabling native code mode, built-in environments, user MCP projection, dynamic tools, and Codex app defaults for restricted runs.
- Collapse plugin-harness deny-all policy into `toolsAllow: []` at runtime, matching PI behavior while preserving the config/source-of-truth shape as `deny: ["*"]`.
- Add sender/chat-specific refusal wording for denied users and keep restricted Codex turns transient so an allowed sender's existing tool-capable thread binding is preserved.

## Root Cause
Telegram sender/chat deny-all policy is configured as `deny: ["*"]`. Plugin harnesses did not consistently collapse that deny-all policy into an empty runtime allowlist before invoking Codex. In the Codex app-server path, an explicit empty allowlist still left native app-server surfaces available: code mode could remain enabled, built-in environments could still register shell/file tools, user MCP servers could still project, and Codex app defaults were not always explicitly disabled. That let a denied sender appear to have `toolCount: 0` while native Codex tool surfaces could still run.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A sender/chat/runtime `deny: ["*"]` policy now produces a restricted Codex run that has no OpenClaw dynamic tools, no Codex native code-mode shell/file environment, no user MCP projection, and explicit Codex app default denial.

Real environment tested: Local temp worktree from current `origin/main`, using the repo Vitest wrapper for the touched harness and Codex app-server lifecycle surfaces. The patched build was also installed into the live gateway container and verified for code presence/health, but no post-patch live Telegram roundtrip was completed yet.

Before proof: In the pre-patch live gateway behavior, a denied-sender request under `deny: ["*"]` could still result in Codex-native shell/file tool calls and a completed edit even though policy intended a no-tool run. The pre-patch code also treated `toolsAllow: []` as an unrestricted dynamic-tool catalog, kept Codex code mode enabled, did not send `environments: []`, and did not always send an explicit `apps._default` denial.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 5 passed (5)` and `Tests 252 passed (252)`.

Observed result after fix: The regression coverage verifies sender-specific `deny: ["*"]` becomes `toolsAllow: []` with sender-policy refusal wording, PI harness params remain unchanged, restricted Codex `thread/start` receives `dynamicTools: []`, `environments: []`, `features.code_mode: false`, `features.code_mode_only: false`, `apps._default` disabled, no `app/list`, no user MCP servers, and transient restricted turns do not overwrite an existing plugin-enabled thread binding.

Live deployment proof: Rebuilt and restarted the live gateway container from this branch. The container reported `running healthy`, and in-container source/dist checks found the sender-specific denial prompt, the restricted-native-tool branch, the explicit `apps._default` denial patch comment, and `environments: []` in the installed build.

What was not tested: A live Telegram roundtrip against the deployed gateway was not completed after this patch. Broad `pnpm check` / full CI was not run locally.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts`
- `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local` (`codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)
- Live gateway container rebuilt/recreated from this branch and verified healthy with patched code present.

## What was not tested
- Post-patch live Telegram roundtrip through the deployed gateway.
- Broad `pnpm check` / full CI locally.
